items.name: Guild Firebomb
items.description: A volatile alchemical device made by the Hollow Hand. Burns hot enough to ignite stone. Handle with extreme care.

items.name: Heavy Gold Pouch
items.description: A leather pouch stuffed with gold coins. Payment for dirty work.

items.name: Nightcap Root Sample
items.description: A dried sample of the fungal root poisoning Ashvale's water. Proof of sabotage.

items.name: Herb Poultice
items.description: A healing salve made from forest herbs. Stops bleeding and dulls pain.

items.name: Stale Bread
items.description: Hard and tasteless, but it fills the stomach. Luxury in times of famine.

items.name: Iron Dagger
items.description: A simple but serviceable blade. Won't win any duels, but better than bare hands.

items.name: Leather Vest
items.description: Worn leather armor. Offers modest protection against claws and blades.

items.name: Manor Key
items.description: An ornate iron key bearing the Ashworth crest. Opens the manor's front gate.

items.name: Torn Cloth Fragment
items.description: A scrap of fine cloth found near the poisoned water source. Bears a partial emblem — but whose?

items.name: Druid's Talisman
items.description: A carved wooden pendant given by Old Maren. She says it will 'show you what the forest wants you to see.'

items.name: Maren's Silver Compass
items.description: A tarnished silver compass that no longer points north. The name 'Silas Maren' is scratched into the back in a child's handwriting. The needle trembles when held near old trees.

items.name: Nightcap Antidote
items.description: A carefully brewed remedy that neutralizes nightcap root poisoning. The mixture smells of clean earth and tastes like forgiveness.

items.name: Druidfire Torch
items.description: A torch that burns with an eerie green flame. Old Maren says it can sever magical bonds and reveal hidden paths. The fire doesn't consume the wood — it feeds on something else entirely.

items.name: Tongueloose Draught
items.description: A murky amber liquid that loosens inhibitions and encourages honesty. Slip it into a drink during conversation for... illuminating results. Elara insists it's not poison. Technically.

items.name: Smoke Bomb
items.description: A compact alchemical device that releases a thick cloud of choking gray smoke. Fenrick's design — compact, reliable, and just unpleasant enough to buy you time.

items.name: Moonpetal Flower
items.description: A delicate silver-white flower that only blooms in the Heartwood Clearing under moonlight. Its petals glow faintly, even after picking. Old Maren says the forest gives these freely — but only to those it trusts.

items.name: Moonpetal Tea
items.description: A luminous tea brewed from moonpetal flowers. Drinking it lets you hear whispers in the wind — the forest's voice, speaking in impressions and emotions rather than words. The effect lasts about an hour.

items.name: Torch
items.description: A simple wooden torch wrapped in oil-soaked cloth. Burns for about four hours. Essential for the catacombs, where the darkness is not merely the absence of light.

items.name: Crypt Key
items.description: A heavy iron key, black with age, found on the body of the dead messenger. The teeth are carved with the Ashworth family motto: 'The Root Remembers.' Opens the sealed section of the catacombs.

items.name: Sacred Text of the Ashen Light
items.description: A leather-bound book of Church doctrine. Most of it is standard religious text, but the final chapter — 'On the Purification of Corrupted Lands' — reads like a military operations manual. Annotated in Brother Maren's handwriting.

items.name: Ashworth Family Ledger
items.description: A crumbling ledger spanning generations of Ashworth lords. Every thirty years, an entry is marked in red ink: 'The Debt Paid.' The payments are never described. The names beside them are never mentioned again.

items.name: Bundle of Firewood
items.description: Dry pine, split and bundled. Essential for cooking and warmth.

items.name: Pouch of Salt
items.description: Coarse salt from the eastern mines. Preserves meat, flavors food, and wards off... something, according to Old Maren.

items.name: Sack of Flour
items.description: Ground wheat. Increasingly rare since the crops failed.

items.name: Fresh Eggs
items.description: From the village's dwindling flock. Three left. Handle with care.

items.name: Dried Fish
items.description: Salted and sun-dried. Harlan's staple. Tastes like discipline and regret.

items.name: Tallow Candles
items.description: Three candles, rendered from what's left of the village tallow. Brother Maren has been buying black ones.

items.name: Rough Wool Cloth
items.description: Undyed wool. Scratchy, warm, honest.

items.name: Iron Nails
items.description: A handful of hand-forged nails. The forge has been cold for months. These are from the last batch.

items.name: Lye Soap
items.description: Hard, alkaline, effective. Greta makes it from ash and animal fat.

items.name: Turnips
items.description: The only crop still growing. Tough, bitter, and stubbornly alive.

items.name: Apple Cider
items.description: Last year's pressing. Slightly sour. The apples won't come again.

items.name: Leather Scraps
items.description: Offcuts from the tanner's last project. Useful for repairs.

items.name: Needle and Thread
items.description: Steel needle with a spool of linen thread. Brin the farmer's wife sews clothes smaller every week.

items.name: Pickled Onions
items.description: Harlan's personal stash. He says they're 'the only thing that makes the job bearable.'

items.name: Charcoal
items.description: From the forge's last firing. Used for drawing, writing, and making things disappear.
